question:
Menstrual cycle lengths (days) in an SRS of
nine women are as follows: {31, 28, 26, 24, 29, 33, 25, 26, 28}. Use this data to test whether mean menstrual cycle length differs significantly from a lunar month. (A lunar month is 29.5 days.) Assume population values vary according to a Normal distribution. Use a twosided alternative. Show all hypothesis-testing steps.
answer:
A. Hypotheses. $H_0: \mu=29.5$ days versus $H_{\mathrm{a}}: \mu 
eq 29.5$ days
B. Test statistic.

$$
 egin{aligned}
& n=9 \
&  ar{x}=27.78 \
& \quad s=2.906 \
& S E_{ ar{x}}= rac{s}{\sqrt{n}}= rac{2.906}{\sqrt{9}}=0.96877 \
& t_{	ext {stat }}= rac{ ar{x}_d-\mu_0}{S E_{ ar{x}_d}}= rac{27.78-29.5}{0.9687}=-1.77 \
& \quad d f=9-1=8
\end{aligned}
$$

C. $ oldsymbol{P}$-value. Use Table C to determine that $t_{8.90}=1.40$ (right tail $=0.10$ ) and $t_{8.95}=$ 1.86 (right tail $=0.05$ ). Therefore, the one-sided $P$-value is between 0.05 and 0.10 and the two-tailed $P$-value is $0.10<P<0.20$. Using a utility program, $P=0.11$.
D. Significance level (optional). The evidence against $H_0$ is not significantly at $\alpha =0.10$ (retain $H_0$ ).
